name: Jeju
description: Korea's Hawaii — a volcanic island of black lava rock beaches, tangerine orchards, diving grandmothers (haenyeo), waterfalls that fall into the sea, and the country's highest peak draped in clouds.

quickInfo.visa: Visa-free entry directly to Jeju for most nationalities (without K-ETA)

hotels.name: Jeju Dream Tower Guest House
hotels.description: Clean guesthouse close to the new Jeju Dream Tower and city bus lines

hotels.name: Jeju Shinhwa World Landing Resort
hotels.description: Mega resort complex with water park, casino, K-drama filming locations, and family facilities

hotels.name: Maison Glad Jeju
hotels.description: Smart hotel with rooftop bar overlooking the sea and Hallasan mountain backdrop

hotels.name: Lotte Hotel Jeju
hotels.description: Iconic white castle hotel on Jungmun's cliff, Korea's premier resort destination

hotels.name: Ananti at Cheongna Jeju
hotels.description: Ultra-luxury cliffside resort with Seopjikoji views and private Korean spa facilities

activities.name: Hallasan National Park Summit Hike
activities.description: Hike to the summit of South Korea's highest peak — a volcano crater lake at 1,947m

activities.name: Haenyeo Diving Experience & Cultural Show
activities.description: Meet Jeju's legendary female free-divers, watch their harvest, and try freshly caught abalone

activities.name: Jeju East Highlights: Seongsan & Manjanggul
activities.description: Visit Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO sunrise crater), Manjanggul lava tube cave, and Hamdeok Beach

activities.name: Jeju Food & Market Tour
activities.description: Black pork BBQ, Jeju tangerines, hallabong, and dongdongju rice wine tasting tour

activities.name: Jeju West Driving Tour: Olle Trail & Cheonjiyeon
activities.description: Walk the famous Olle coastal path, visit Cheonjiyeon waterfall and Jusangjeolli lava columns

items.activity: Haenyeo village breakfast: fresh sea catches
Black pork BBQ dinner in Jeju City
Hallasan summit hike via Seongpanak trail
Descent, hot spring soak in Yeomiji
Seafood dinner: abalone porridge at a local restaurant
Iho Tewoo Beach — white and black sand
Farewell: Hallabong tangerine dessert & jeju soju

practicalInfo.gettingThere: Jeju International Airport (CJU) is Korea's busiest domestic route from Seoul (1 hour, flights every 30 min). Some direct international flights from Tokyo, Osaka, Shanghai, Hong Kong, and Taipei.
practicalInfo.localTransport: Car rental essential to see the island properly (~$35-50/day). Intercity buses exist but slow. Taxis plentiful. E-bikes and scooters available to rent. Some attractions walkable via Jeju Olle trails.
